message:
I've spent half my life being the person who *forgets* the umbrella, shows up late to things, and generally wings it. So when that winter storm warning popped up last week, I actually—get this—*checked our supplies* the night before. Flashlights? Checked. Battery backup for phones? There. Extra blankets? Yep. And I gotta say, there's something quietly nice about not scrambling at the last minute. Nothing fancy, just... ready.

My partner actually asked if I'd been body-snatched. 😄 Turns out being the prepared one for once feels kind of good—less stress, more "I got this."

Do you usually prep ahead for storms, or are you more of a last-minute person like old-me?
